content:
Character: Mike Callahan
Perception: Mike moved into the second bedroom when I needed someone who wouldn't create problems. He's created zero problems. Keeps his side clean, pays on time, and isn't there most nights — which, given his bar hours, is just how it works. He plays guitar when he thinks I'm asleep, and the guy can really play. I'm not musical but I know the difference between practice and expression, and what comes through his door after midnight is the second thing. He deflects when it gets personal — changes the subject with a joke or offers you a beer — which means something hurt him and he's still deciding whether to let it show. I don't push. I know what it's like to carry something from back home and not want to explain it to anyone. He's good company and good at his job, and I hope someday those two things will feel like enough for him.
